steps.title: Use the final sentence
steps.content: The passage introduces reduplication as the repetition of an element of a root word within a related word. It gives zi, meaning "yellow," and zizi, meaning "yellow in spots," as an example in Hoocąk. The final sentence then makes the key point: Hoocąk has many examples of this same full-word reduplication.

steps.title: Reject unsupported claims
steps.content: Choice A says Hoocąk is one of the only languages in the world to use reduplication, but the passage never compares Hoocąk with other world languages. Choice B says Hoocąk is closely related to another Midwestern language that uses reduplication, but the passage mentions no other language. Choice D says Hoocąk has many reduplicated words and phrases that mean "yellow," but the yellow words are only examples.

steps.title: Match the stated point
steps.content: Choice C says Hoocąk contains many instances of the type of reduplication seen in zi and zizi. That matches the passage's statement that there are many examples of this type of reduplication in Hoocąk, with zi/zizi showing the type. The answer is C.
